mission: Learn transferable production-agent engineering by reproducing failures, naming contracts, choosing deliberately, and proving repairs.

phases.name: Measure
phases.promise: Trust the instrument and define the service promise.

phases.name: Isolate
phases.promise: Make sessions concurrent without crossing tenant boundaries.

phases.name: Operate under pressure
phases.promise: Control state, demand, provider failure, and spend.

phases.name: Run as a service
phases.promise: Observe, deploy, and roll out the agent safely.

phases.name: Define agent behavior
phases.promise: Make contracts, authority, and coordination explicit.

phases.name: Survive production reality
phases.promise: Govern context, hosting, and durable execution.

knowledge_checks: Which measurement proves a full conversation traversed the real public API?
What false confidence would a mocked inner loop create?

lab.scenario: Prove the public Waku path works before trusting any later scale result.
objectives: Run five simulated tenants through the real harness.
Inspect the public-path evidence rather than an inner-loop mock.
Explain which correctness invariant the smoke test establishes.

knowledge_checks: Which product promise does each SLO protect, and for what workload boundary?
What evidence distinguishes queueing collapse from a merely slow model?

lab.scenario: Watch a single process-wide lock flatten throughput as tenant concurrency rises.
objectives: Reproduce the baseline latency ramp before changing code.
Name the workload boundary and service promise.
Capture evidence that distinguishes queueing from model latency.

knowledge_checks: What must remain ordered inside one session while other sessions progress?
Where can concurrency be introduced without duplicating effects?

lab.scenario: Ramp to 50 tenants and isolate one deliberately slow session from its neighbors.
objectives: Measure cross-session blocking under concurrent turns.
Preserve ordering inside each individual session.
Prove one slow session no longer delays unrelated tenants.

knowledge_checks: Where is tenant identity constructed, and why can the model not choose it?
Which stores, tools, traces, and meters must a leak test cover?

lab.scenario: Seed private tenant facts, retrieve them under load, and hunt for cross-tenant leakage.
objectives: Reproduce a private-fact leak across tenants.
Trace trusted tenant identity through every state boundary.
Prove an abusive tenant cannot degrade its neighbors.

knowledge_checks: Which state must survive process loss, and which state may remain ephemeral?
What query or migration fails first at the next order of magnitude?

lab.scenario: Operate against a fat database while dashboard reads and agent writes compete.
objectives: Identify the first query that collapses at production volume.
Measure dashboard polling separately from turn latency.
Prove migration and restart preserve authoritative state.

knowledge_checks: Where should overload be rejected so work does not enter the system dishonestly?
How do deadlines and retries interact when the queue is already saturated?

lab.scenario: Send a 10x burst into a bounded worker pool and observe the overload death spiral.
objectives: Distinguish admitted, queued, expired, and rejected work.
Keep admitted requests inside the latency promise.
Prove overload rejects quickly with actionable retry evidence.

knowledge_checks: Which failures are safe to retry, and what budget stops amplification?
What evidence tells a real recovery from a fallback that violates the contract?

lab.scenario: Inject provider errors, rate limits, and hangs while normal traffic continues.
objectives: Classify failures by retry safety.
Observe retry budgets and circuit-breaker state.
Prove fallback recovery still honors the service contract.

knowledge_checks: Where must a shared budget be enforced to cover model and tool work together?
What happens to an in-flight run when its tenant budget is exhausted?

lab.scenario: Mix normal tenants with a flooder, context abuser, and tool-loop baiter.
objectives: Attribute model, retry, fallback, and tool spend to tenants.
Stop a run when its shared budget is exhausted.
Prove abusers cannot change normal-tenant latency or cost.

knowledge_checks: Which correlation identifiers let you follow one accepted request across boundaries?
How can telemetry localize failure without leaking prompt or tenant content?

lab.scenario: Diagnose a hidden fault from telemetry alone against a running clock.
objectives: Follow one run across gateway, queue, model, and tool boundaries.
Localize the injected fault without reading test source.
Prove observability reveals control flow without leaking content.

knowledge_checks: Which accepted work becomes unsafe if a worker disappears mid-request?
What hidden affinity would make a rolling restart lose correctness?

lab.scenario: Route sessions across multiple workers and terminate one during active work.
objectives: Expose hidden worker affinity in session state.
Kill a worker without losing completed turns.
Prove rolling replacement preserves ordering and isolation.

knowledge_checks: Which offline and live signals should automatically block promotion?
How do shadow and canary traffic answer different rollout questions?

lab.scenario: Promote a deliberately worse agent configuration and make the rollout system object.
objectives: Compare candidate and control behavior with shadow traffic.
Route a bounded live canary without side effects.
Prove automatic rollback blocks a defined regression.

knowledge_checks: At which boundaries must invalid agent data fail explicitly?
What compatibility promise does a contract version make to producers and consumers?

lab.scenario: Send malformed and old-version envelopes through every agent boundary.
objectives: Reject invalid data at the first named boundary.
Keep supported older envelopes compatible.
Prove generated schemas match runtime validation.

knowledge_checks: Which values may inform a decision but must never grant capability?
Where must approval be verified so prompt injection cannot forge it?

lab.scenario: Forge tenant identity and approval claims through every model-visible channel.
objectives: Separate prompt-visible data from trusted run authority.
Bind approval to the exact proposed action.
Prove every forged path is denied and audited.

knowledge_checks: Which handoff fields make specialist work inspectable and resumable?
Where is the terminal state decided when specialists disagree or exhaust budget?

lab.scenario: Run a staged specialist workflow with ambiguous handoffs and a shared budget.
objectives: Expose duplicate or forbidden delegation.
Carry typed artifacts between deterministic stages.
Prove every run reaches one explainable terminal state.

knowledge_checks: What provenance, freshness, and scope must accompany retrieved context?
Which write path could let generated text poison future decisions?

lab.scenario: Seed stale, poisoned, conflicting, and cross-tenant records into retrieval.
objectives: Make provenance and freshness visible in ranking.
Block poisoned and cross-tenant context from authority.
Prove memory writes are attributable and reversible.

knowledge_checks: Which startup and shutdown behaviors must be proved inside the immutable image?
What local dependency would fail under non-root and read-only production constraints?

lab.scenario: Run the production image non-root and read-only, then terminate it under load.
objectives: Prove health and readiness mean different things.
Eliminate baked secrets and worker-local durable state.
Prove SIGTERM drains accepted work inside its budget.

knowledge_checks: After which side effects can a worker die, and what guarantee holds at each point?
What idempotency key and durable state allow duplicate delivery to resume safely?

lab.scenario: Crash workers around claims, side effects, checkpoints, and acknowledgement.
objectives: Map every crash point to an explicit owner and recovery path.
Fence stale claimants and deduplicate external effects.
Prove replay and cancellation converge on one terminal result.
